response:
The task requires translating a French sentence into English. This process involves understanding the nuances of the original language and accurately conveying the meaning in the target language. Translating is not just about word-for-word conversion but also about preserving the intended message and tone. It is essential to consider context and cultural references to ensure the translation is both accurate and natural-sounding.

Assumption is that the French sentence provided is straightforward and does not contain idiomatic expressions that may complicate the translation. However it is important to note that not all sentences can be translated directly without some level of interpretation. The translator must be aware of the differences in grammar and syntax between the two languages to produce a coherent and meaningful translation.

The sentence "Inutile j'ai obtenu un rendu" translates to "No need I got one made." This translation captures the essence of the original sentence while maintaining clarity and fluency in English. It is crucial to ensure that the translated sentence reads naturally to an English speaker and conveys the same sentiment as the original.

When translating sentences from one language to another it is important to maintain the original intent and meaning. This involves not only understanding the literal meaning of the words but also the context in which they are used. Translators must be careful to avoid literal translations that may result in awkward or confusing phrasing in the target language.

The sentence "Le but du design du pavillon était d'être calme et tranquille" translates to "The purpose of the pavilion's design was to be calm and quiet." This translation effectively conveys the intended meaning while maintaining the descriptive quality of the original sentence. It is important to ensure that the translated sentence is both accurate and natural-sounding in the target language.

Time flies when having fun  
Time flies when enjoying fun  

P.S. The translation process requires attention to detail and a deep understanding of both languages. It is important to ensure that the translated sentence is not only accurate but also natural-sounding to the target audience. Translators must be able to convey the intended meaning while maintaining the original tone and style of the sentence.
